question_text: Explain why a ball bounced on the ground doesn't return to exactly the same height it was dropped from.
correct_answer: Energy is lost due to friction/air resistance and sound/deformation during the collision with the ground. This energy cannot be used to lift the ball back to its original height.
explanation: Although total energy is conserved, some of the ball's mechanical energy is transformed into heat, sound, and deformation energy during impact. This energy is no longer available to lift the ball upward.
